analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 7 finding(s) from 9 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Debug-originated control/access paths appear reachable without a top-level jtag_unlock/authentication gate.
findings.vulnerability_category: trigger DMI/debug requests, hart debug request/reset/activity, and debug-module slave accesses / debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_slave_* path into dm_top, debug NoC AXI-lite path / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external JTAG pins, SimDTM/SimJTAG, and NoC debug requester Operation: trigger DMI/debug requests, hart debug request/reset/activity, and debug-module slave accesses Object: debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_slave_* path into dm_top, debug NoC AXI-lite path Expected guard: JTAG/debug requests should be qualified by jtag_unlock or equivalent debug authentication/lifecycle enable before driving dm_top or debug NoC access. Observed behavior: dmi_jtag receives jtag_hash/okey_hash/ikey_hash and outputs jtag_unlock, but debug_req/debug_req_valid from dmi_jtag are connected to the debug module path without an explicit top-level jtag_unlock gate. SimDTM can drive debug_req_valid/debug_req in simulation without visible jtag_unlock. The debug NoC bridge drives dm_master AXI, converted by axi2mem to dm_slave_req/we/addr for the debug module, without observed acct_ctrl/reglk/jtag_unlock guard. Path: tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i or SimDTM/SimJTAG -> dmi_jtag/SimDTM -> debug_req_valid/debug_req -> dm_top -> debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o; buf_ariane_debug_noc2_* -> noc_axilite_bridge -> dm_master -> axi2mem -> dm_slave_* -> dm_top Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Unauthenticated or insufficiently authorized debug requesters may activate debug functionality, request hart debug/reset behavior, or access debug module registers, potentially bypassing privilege isolation and enabling control of hart execution.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Exact dm_top instantiation lines are only partially represented in the observations; debug module internal handling of jtag_unlock is not shown.

findings.summary: Wrapper lock/access controls are present but inconsistently applied; PKT fuse address/request writes lack lock checks and RNG debug mode bypasses lock-protected read/write controls.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/configure software-visible peripheral registers / RNG seed/poly/output/debug-state registers, RSA control/operand/result registers, PKT fuse request/address/readback/pkey_loc registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software or NoC AXI-lite requester reaching rng_wrapper, rsa_wrapper, and pkt_wrapper Operation: read/write/configure software-visible peripheral registers Object: RNG seed/poly/output/debug-state registers, RSA control/operand/result registers, PKT fuse request/address/readback/pkey_loc registers Expected guard: Protected register writes should require acct_ctrl_i and appropriate reglk_ctrl_i unlocked state; sensitive reads should be blocked unless access policy permits; debug paths should not bypass locks without authorization. Observed behavior: Wrappers gate local en with acct_ctrl_i, but lock use is inconsistent. pkt_wrapper writes fuse_req_o and fuse_addr_o under en && we without reglk_ctrl_i checks. rng_wrapper read paths return seed/poly/internal state when debug_mode_i is high regardless of reglk_ctrl_i and write paths for polynomial registers also use debug_mode_i as a lock bypass. rsa_wrapper uses reglk_ctrl_i[3] for many RSA writes/reads but relies on one broad bit and does not demonstrate debug authorization semantics. Path: NoC endpoint -> noc_axilite_bridge in riscv_peripherals -> wrapper axi_lite_interface -> en=en_acct&&acct_ctrl_i -> address case write/read decodes Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A requester with access-control enable, or a requester able to force/enter debug mode, may modify protected configuration or read sensitive state despite register locks, weakening downstream protection of crypto/fuse/RNG assets.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Exact intended semantics of debug_mode_i and reglk bit assignments are not provided; wrapper generation of access-control policy is outside the inspected excerpts.

findings.summary: PKT exposes requester-controlled fuse address/request and fuse read data with insufficient lock/range mediation.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/expose fuse data and key-location data / fuse_mem contents, jtag_hash_o, fuse_rdata, pkey_loc_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software/NoC requester using pkt peripheral Operation: read/expose fuse data and key-location data Object: fuse_mem contents, jtag_hash_o, fuse_rdata, pkey_loc_o Expected guard: Fuse reads and private-key-location outputs should be restricted by access-control, lock state, privilege/debug authorization, or a narrow valid address range. Observed behavior: pkt_wrapper allows an acct_ctrl_i-enabled requester to write fuse_req_o and fuse_addr_o without reglk checks. Its read mux can return fuse_rdata_i at address 4 when reglk_ctrl_i[6] is clear, and defaults rdata to fuse_rdata_i before the case. pkey_loc words are returned when reglk_ctrl_i[4]/[5] are clear. pkt instance is driven with req_i tied to 1'b1 and fuse_indx_i=fuse_addr_o. fuse_mem contains security-sensitive constants including JTAG hash, HMAC keys, SHA/AES keys, access-control data, and exposes rdata_o indexed by addr_i on req_i. Path: buf_ariane_pkt_noc2_* -> noc_axilite_bridge -> pkt_axi_req -> pkt_wrapper -> fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o -> fuse_mem req_i/addr_i -> fuse_rdata -> pkt_wrapper read response; fuse_addr_o -> pkt -> pkey_loc_o -> pkt_wrapper read response Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Authorized-to-PKT or misclassified requesters may enumerate fuse contents or key-location mappings, exposing cryptographic keys, access-control policy words, and debug authentication hashes.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The exact fuse_mem read always_ff lines after 127 are truncated, so the precise out-of-range behavior is not fully closed.

findings.summary: RNG seed/configuration/internal state are readable or writable through MMIO when debug_mode_i bypasses register locks.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure seed/polynomial, load RNG state, read RNG output or internal state / RNG seed_i, poly*_i, seed128_o/seed64_o/seed32_o/seed16_o, rand segments, cs_state_o, rand_num_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software/NoC requester to rng_wrapper or debug-mode path Operation: configure seed/polynomial, load RNG state, read RNG output or internal state Object: RNG seed_i, poly*_i, seed128_o/seed64_o/seed32_o/seed16_o, rand segments, cs_state_o, rand_num_o Expected guard: RNG seed, polynomial, load/configuration registers and internal state outputs should be protected by reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i and not exposed through debug unless explicitly authorized. Observed behavior: rng_wrapper sets en=en_acct&&acct_ctrl_i, but read mux returns seed, polynomial, output seed state, rand segments, and cs_state_o when debug_mode_i is high regardless of corresponding reglk bits. Polynomial writes also allow debug_mode_i to bypass reglk protection. Seed reset/load logic uses fixed constants in the observed excerpt, and rng_top exposes internal seed/state outputs connected into wrapper readback. Path: NoC RNG endpoint -> noc_axilite_bridge -> rng_wrapper axi_lite_interface -> en/read/write decodes -> rng_top load_i/seed_i/poly*_i and output readback to AXI response Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A debug-mode requester with RNG peripheral access can disclose RNG seed/internal state or alter polynomials, making generated random numbers predictable or attacker-controlled.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full RNG write decode from lines 128-145 and rng_top port connections after line 220 are truncated.

findings.summary: Security-sensitive NoC endpoint bridges appear to route requests to peripheral wrappers using only endpoint selection and acct_ctrl/priv_lvl-derived wrapper enables, without requester-attribute propagation.
findings.vulnerability_category: route read/write transaction to peripheral endpoint / peripheral address map and access to debug/reglk/acct/rst/crypto/fuse/DMA endpoints / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: NoC or AXI master accessing riscv_peripherals Operation: route read/write transaction to peripheral endpoint Object: peripheral address map and access to debug/reglk/acct/rst/crypto/fuse/DMA endpoints Expected guard: Routing to debug, reset, crypto, fuse/pkt, policy, RNG, and DMA endpoints should preserve address-range checks and requester attributes; privileged/debug-only endpoints should not be reachable from generic NoC inputs without policy checks. Observed behavior: riscv_peripherals exposes separate NoC ingress/egress ports for debug, reset, PKT, acct, reglk, DMA, RNG, RSA and other peripherals. noc_axilite_bridge instances pass endpoint NoC traffic into local AXI-lite requests. Observed AXI-lite interface returns OK responses and does not enforce errors; wrapper enables use acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral_index], where priv_lvl_i is a top-level input rather than a per-requester attribute in the observed bridge. Debug bridge to dm_master/dm_slave lacks acct_ctrl/reglk gating. Path: NoC endpoint ports -> per-endpoint noc_axilite_bridge -> wrapper/dm AXI request -> local peripheral decode/read/write Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A generic NoC master that can inject traffic on or be routed to a sensitive endpoint may reach debug, reset, fuse/PKT, policy, crypto, or DMA registers without per-requester privilege attributes, enabling unauthorized control or data disclosure.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Top-level NoC splitter/address mapping outside the provided excerpts is not shown; requester identity semantics for dedicated NoC ports are unknown.

findings.summary: Top-level declares reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl, derives acc_ctrl_c from acc_ctrl indexed by priv_lvl_i, routes acct_wrapper output acc_ctrl_o to acc_ctrl and reglk_wrapper likely supplies reglk_ctrl. acct_wrapper is connected to reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i, and reglk_wrapper is mentioned as receiving jtag_unlock in prior observations, but the internal write/clear/reset semantics of acct_wrapper/reglk_wrapper are not provided.
findings.vulnerability_category: write policy bit, clear lock, change access control / reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl policy registers/signals / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software/NoC requester to access-control/register-lock peripheral Operation: write policy bit, clear lock, change access control Object: reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl policy registers/signals Expected guard: Policy updates should be protected by higher-level permission and sticky lock semantics; once locked, lock/access policy should not be writable or clearable by unauthorized requesters. Observed behavior: Top-level declares reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl, derives acc_ctrl_c from acc_ctrl indexed by priv_lvl_i, routes acct_wrapper output acc_ctrl_o to acc_ctrl and reglk_wrapper likely supplies reglk_ctrl. acct_wrapper is connected to reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i, and reglk_wrapper is mentioned as receiving jtag_unlock in prior observations, but the internal write/clear/reset semantics of acct_wrapper/reglk_wrapper are not provided. Path: buf_ariane_acct_noc2_* and buf_ariane_reglk_noc2_* -> noc_axilite_bridge -> acct_wrapper/reglk_wrapper -> acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl -> downstream wrapper guards Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If policy registers are writable or clearable without a higher-level/sticky guard, all downstream acct/reglk protections for crypto, fuse, reset, DMA, and debug-adjacent endpoints could be disabled; however, the provided evidence does not prove such a write path.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Internal acct_wrapper/reglk_wrapper RTL, reset defaults, write masks, clear conditions, and sticky lock semantics are not included in the observations.

findings.summary: Top-level instantiates dma_wrapper connected to reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, pmpcfg_i, pmpaddr_i, and we_flag, and exposes a dedicated NoC AXI-lite bridge to dma_axi_req/resp. The internal dma_wrapper implementation, transfer datapath, address bounds checks, and active-transfer update behavior are not provided.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure DMA and perform read/write transfer / memory/peripheral address space reachable by DMA, especially crypto/fuse/debug/policy endpoints / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: DMA requester via ariane_dma NoC interface Operation: configure DMA and perform read/write transfer Object: memory/peripheral address space reachable by DMA, especially crypto/fuse/debug/policy endpoints Expected guard: DMA configuration and transfers should be constrained by PMP/range/access-control checks equivalent to CPU policy, and active-transfer configuration updates should not bypass bounds. Observed behavior: Top-level instantiates dma_wrapper connected to reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, pmpcfg_i, pmpaddr_i, and we_flag, and exposes a dedicated NoC AXI-lite bridge to dma_axi_req/resp. The internal dma_wrapper implementation, transfer datapath, address bounds checks, and active-transfer update behavior are not provided. Path: buf_ariane_dma_noc2_* -> noc_axilite_bridge -> dma_axi_req/resp -> dma_wrapper with pmpcfg_i/pmpaddr_i Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If dma_wrapper fails to enforce PMP/range/access policy, DMA could bypass CPU privilege checks and access crypto/fuse/debug/policy endpoints; available evidence only shows intended guard inputs, not enforcement.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: dma_wrapper RTL and actual transfer request path are not included, so address permission enforcement cannot be determined.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
